title: ‘It feels like home’: Chinatown celebrates the Year of the Horse
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chinatown's Lunar New Year parade featured 10 lion dance troupes despite snowy weather, celebrating the Year of the Horse.
The event was organized by Chinatown Main Street and the Chinese Consolidated Benevolent Association of New England.
Gund Kwok, an all-women lion and dragon dance troupe, performed with a unique horse costume element.

title: Boston 25 News promotes Shiri Spear to chief meteorologist
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Shiri Spear has been promoted to chief meteorologist at Boston 25 News, succeeding Kevin Lemanowicz.
Spear will lead the station's weather coverage, severe weather strategy, and community outreach across all platforms.
Spear holds a bachelor's in mathematics education and a master's in atmospheric science and is a certified broadcast meteorologist.

title: R.I. prosecutor who told police ‘I’m an AG’ during arrest is back at work
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Devon Flanagan, a Rhode Island prosecutor, is back at work after pleading no contest to misdemeanor trespassing.
Flanagan had been on unpaid leave since her August arrest in Newport and has been reassigned to Providence County Superior Court.
The prosecutor's annual salary is approximately $117,000, and her return followed public censure from Attorney General Peter Neronha.

title: Should pajamas be banned at Logan Airport? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston.com polled 265 readers on banning pajamas at Logan Airport; 48% support a ban, 42% oppose, 11% were conditional.
Comments highlight issues of respect and public decency with some arguing pajamas are unsuitable for public spaces like airports.
Others argue comfort is essential during travel, citing airport stress and personal choice in attire, opposing any formal ban.

title: ‘A crisis of Trump’s creation’: Mass. politicians respond to strikes on Iran
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts politicians criticize Trump's strikes on Iran, calling for congressional votes on war powers resolutions this week.
Sen. Elizabeth Warren and Sen. Ed Markey condemn the military actions as illegal and urge diplomatic solutions to prevent further conflict.
Rep. Katherine Clark links military actions to domestic issues like rising costs and ICE crackdowns, highlighting public opposition to war.

title: Carmelo Anthony, Tracy McGrady impressed with Celtics after win vs. 76ers
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics beat 76ers 114-98, impressing former NBA players Carmelo Anthony and Tracy McGrady with their strategic play.
Jaylen Brown and Neemias Queta each scored 27 points, leading the Celtics in their victory over Philadelphia.
Boston will play the Milwaukee Bucks tonight at 7:30 p.m. in their next NBA match.

title: Fatal fire under investigation in Roxbury
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A fire at 4 Ellingwood St. in Roxbury left one person dead, discovered by firefighters on Friday afternoon.
Boston Police and Fire Departments are investigating the two-alarm blaze for potential arson and homicide connections.
The fire was controlled shortly after firefighters arrived around 3 p.m., affecting all three floors of the building.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in March
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Bambola, a Seaport supper club, offers Roman and Southern Italian cuisine by Bartolo Bruzzaniti, opening late March.
Common Craft South Boston features a rotating culinary theme and a speakeasy, led by chef Tony Messina, opening mid-March.
Lanikai, a Hawaiian poke pop-up in Cambridge, serves sushi hand rolls with poke ingredients, now open at Canal Park.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about Tuesday’s ‘wintry mix’
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: National Weather Service warns of a wintry mix impacting travel with snow, sleet, and freezing rain on Tuesday.
Meteorologists forecast light snow turning to rain Tuesday afternoon with temperatures rising to mid-upper 30s.
Another round of rain is expected late Thursday into Friday with milder temperatures reaching the 50s this weekend.

title: Neemias Queta played one of his best games as Celtics pull away late vs. 76ers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Neemias Queta scored 27 points with 17 rebounds, leading the Celtics to a 114-98 win over the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown had a LeBron-esque performance with 27 points, eight rebounds, and eight assists, showcasing strong passing skills.
The Celtics improved rebounding performance continued, grabbing 59 total rebounds in the game against Philadelphia.
